title: Sentinel Shield tool policy
description: Canonical per-tool policy object and the profile-level 'tools' map. A profile declares, per tool key (matching the TOOL_TABLE keys in scripts/build-security-summary.sh, e.g. phpstan, tests, gitleaks), HOW the tool is obtained, run, and gated. The missing-tool state machine is defined in docs/profile-tool-policy.md and MUST be honored: required+unavailable = config failure; required+execution-error = gate failure; recommended+unavailable = warning; optional+unavailable = info; zero findings ONLY after successful execution; an unavailable tool is NEVER converted to a 0/clean report. $defs.toolPolicy is $ref'd by profiles/profile.manifest.schema.json (the manifest 'tools' field) and schemas/tool-policy-override.schema.json (project overrides, policy-only).

toolKey.description: Stable tool identifier matching a TOOL_TABLE key in scripts/build-security-summary.sh (e.g. phpstan, psalm, tests, gitleaks, third-party-semgrep, github-actions-pins).

policy.description: required: must be present, configured, and pass — gates the build. recommended: should be present; absence warns. optional: nice-to-have; absence is informational. one-of: this tool satisfies a requirement together with its 'alternatives' (e.g. pest|phpunit); at least one of the set must be present+pass. disabled: deliberately turned off for this profile/project. external: provided/run outside Sentinel Shield (results may be imported); not installed or executed by the runner. Precedence when merged/resolved: required > recommended > optional > disabled.

missingBehavior.description: What happens when the tool is unavailable (not detected / no valid report). Typically derived from 'policy' (required->fail, recommended->warn, optional->info, disabled/external->skip) but may be set explicitly to override.

scope.description: Dependency scope: dev (require-dev / devDependencies) or prod (runtime).

compatibility.description: 'auto' to let the installer pick a version compatible with the project's language/framework runtime, or an explicit version constraint string (e.g. '^2.0', '>=1.10 <2').

execution.description: Which CI phases run this tool. Absent keys default to false.

config.description: The tool's project-local configuration file and how Sentinel Shield treats it.

classification.description: managed: Sentinel Shield owns/overwrites it. create-if-missing: written once, then project-owned. manual-template: printed for the maintainer to place by hand. never-touch: must never be created or overwritten. project-owned: exists and is owned by the project; read-only to the installer.

toolPolicy.description: One tool's declared policy within a profile (or a policy-only override in a project tool-policy.yaml).

packages.description: Packages that provide this tool, with install scope and version compatibility.

executable.description: Ordered candidate executables; the runner picks the first that exists, e.g. ['vendor/bin/phpstan','phpstan'].

runner.description: Path to the runner script that produces the normalized report, e.g. 'scripts/runners/laravel-phpstan.sh'.

report.description: Normalized JSON report path under reports/raw/, e.g. 'reports/raw/phpstan.json'.

requires.description: Other tool keys that must be present for this tool to be meaningful (hard prerequisites).

alternatives.description: For policy=one-of: the sibling tool keys that together satisfy a single requirement (e.g. tests: alternatives [pest, phpunit]).

toolsMap.description: Map of tool key -> toolPolicy. This is the shape of a profile-level 'tools' object.
